commit.message:
feat: comprehensive 3-tier test strategy — 161 tests + CI gate

Tier 1 — Unit tests (vitest, 137 tests, 2s):
- graph.test.ts: buildGraph, buildEdges, computeRelated, getHubNodeId
- nodemap.test.ts: all 5 mapping modes, connection derivation, edge cases
- identity.test.ts: assignIdentity URNs, shareIdentity, buildIdentityIndex
- types/index.test.ts: filterGraphToLayer, collapseGraphClusters, getNodeLayer
- providers.test.ts: ProviderRegistry topological sort
- providers/*: FilesProvider, AuthoredProvider, WorkProvider
- Fixed pre-existing parser test failures

Tier 2 — Graph validation (scripts/validate-graph.js, 6 rules):
- No broken inline links, duplicate IDs, orphan authored nodes
- Cluster validity, nodemap file existence, empty content check

Tier 3 — E2E smoke tests (Playwright, 18 tests, 2.4m):
- smoke.spec.ts: app loads, README/overview render, no console errors
- layer-views.spec.ts: All/Files/Content/Work view switching
- navigation.spec.ts: inline link nav, back button, connection card, 404
- display-modes.spec.ts: code/tree/prose display rendering
- cluster-collapse.spec.ts: collapse/expand cycle, strikethrough style

CI/CD:
- github-pages.yml: test job gates deploy (unit → validate → E2E)
- PR trigger added for test-on-PR
- Playwright report uploaded as artifact

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add E2E tests for navigation, display modes, and cluster collapse

- navigation.spec.ts: inline link nav, back button, connection card nav, 404
- display-modes.spec.ts: code display, tree display, prose markdown rendering
- cluster-collapse.spec.ts: collapse/expand toggle, strikethrough style, restore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: code review fixes + test updates

- Edge dedup in collapseGraphClusters now includes type in key
  (was losing multi-type edges between same node pair)
- Summary node related map aggregated from constituent nodes
  (was empty since summary IDs don't exist in original graph)
- Nodemap import resolution prefers authored over file nodes
  for same path (avoids ambiguous resolution)
- Fixed parser tests: expect derived_from edge + identity URN
  on parsed nodes (18 tests passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: provider system + identity URNs (#42, #47)

Graph Provider Architecture:
- GraphProvider interface + ProviderRegistry with topological sort
- FilesProvider wraps treeToNodes, AuthoredProvider wraps parseMarkdownFile
  + loadNodeMap, WorkProvider wraps issues/PRs/commits
- Orchestrator runs providers in dependency order → unified KBGraph
- provider field on KBNode tracks which provider created each node

Identity URNs:
- identity field on KBNode with canonical URN (urn:file:, urn:content:,
  urn:issue:, urn:pr:, urn:commit:)
- assignIdentity() auto-assigns based on node source type
- buildIdentityIndex() maps URNs to all node IDs sharing them
- Content layer filter is now identity-aware: edges from file nodes
  sharing identity with content nodes get remapped to the content node

Files created:
- src/engine/providers.ts — interface + registry
- src/engine/providers/files-provider.ts
- src/engine/providers/authored-provider.ts
- src/engine/providers/work-provider.ts
- src/engine/orchestrator.ts
- src/engine/identity.ts

Closes #42, closes #47

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: node mapping — nodemap.yaml maps files to graph nodes (#41)

New nodemap.yaml config defines how repo files become knowledge graph
nodes with 5 mapping modes:

- **Single file** — 1 file → 1 node (code/prose display)
- **Split** — 1 file → parent + section nodes at ## headings
- **Merge** — N files → 1 combined node
- **Glob** — pattern → N nodes, one per matching file
- **Directory** — dir → 1 tree node with file listing

Display modes in ReadingView:
- code: syntax-highlighted source in <pre><code>
- tree: directory listing with 📁/📄 icons and sizes
- file-list: tabular file listing
- table: pipe/tab-delimited data as HTML table
- diagram: placeholder for future Mermaid support
- prose: existing markdown rendering (default)

Implementation:
- src/engine/nodemap.ts: loadNodeMap() with all 5 modes + connection
  derivation (imports/references/explicit)
- src/types/index.ts: DisplayMode, NodeMapEntry, NodeMap types +
  KBNode.display field
- src/views/ReadingView.tsx: renderContent() dispatcher + TreeView,
  FileListView, TableView helper components
- scripts/generate-manifest.js: reads nodemap.yaml, collects file
  contents + directory listings into manifest
- src/engine/local-loader.ts: processes nodemap from manifest
- nodemap.yaml: 5 demo entries for kbexplorer itself (ESLint, SWA
  config, styles dir, hooks glob, tsconfig merge)

Existing authored content and README unchanged.

Closes #41

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: cluster collapse — click legend to collapse/expand clusters (#57)

Click any cluster name in the sidebar or overlay legend to collapse it
into a single summary node. Collapsed clusters show with strikethrough
text and 50% opacity. Click again to expand. State persisted in
localStorage.

Implementation:
- collapseGraphClusters() in types/index.ts: replaces cluster's nodes
  with one summary node, remaps edges, deduplicates cross-cluster edges
- Collapse state piped through filteredGraph memo (layer → collapse)
- Legend uses the pre-collapse graph so collapsed clusters stay visible
  for toggling back
- Works in both sidebar and overlay legends

Closes #57

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: README with 88 inline links + extract links from README in parser

The README now links every feature, concept, and component it mentions
to the corresponding content node — 88 internal links across 35 unique
targets. Previously the README had zero links to content nodes.

Key changes:
- README.md: card grid → overview-view, constellation → graph-network,
  reading view → reading-view, HUD → hud, GitHub API → github-api,
  local mode → local-loader, manifest → manifest-generator, etc.
- parser.ts + local-loader.ts: README inline link extraction added —
  [text](node-id) links in README.md now create graph edges, same as
  authored content. Previously only fuzzy title matching was used.
- graph.ts: hub node prefers readme (reverted overview-first change)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: default landing page is now 'overview' instead of 'readme'

getHubNodeId() now prefers the authored 'overview' node (the enriched
architecture page with 24 inline links) over the repo 'readme' (the
GitHub-facing install doc). Falls back to readme, then most-connected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

content: enrich README + fix orphans — readability & reachability pass

README (overview.md) expanded from 8 outgoing links to 24, covering
all major graph hubs across six new sections: data flow, visual
experience, graph engine, architecture roadmap, and getting started.

Fixed 4 orphan nodes (0 incoming links):
- catalogue-transformer ← content-pipeline, manifest-generator
- spec-typed-edges ← type-system, graph-engine
- wiki-deep-dive ← wiki-getting-started
- wiki-getting-started ← wiki-overview

Zero orphans remaining. Every content node is now reachable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: font scaling — relative units for headings + line-height, cap at 1.6rem

Headings now use em units (1.65em, 1.35em, 1.12em, 1em) so they scale
with --prose-font-size. Line-height switched from fixed 20px to
relative 1.5 (body) / 1.3 (headings) — no more text overlap at large
sizes. Max font capped at 1.6rem (was 2.6rem which was unreadable).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: convert 204 frontmatter connections to inline links across 42 files

Every frontmatter connections: entry has been replaced with an inline
[text](target-id) link woven naturally into the prose body. The parser
extracts these as 'references' edges (source: 'inline') instead of
'frontmatter' edges — a stronger, more discoverable link type.

Before: 204 frontmatter connections, 0 inline links
After:  0 frontmatter connections, 248 inline links

42 content files modified, net -338 lines (frontmatter YAML is verbose,
inline links are concise).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: double max font size (1.3rem → 2.6rem)

Extended FONT_SIZES array from 5 to 7 steps: 0.92, 1.0, 1.18, 1.4,
1.7, 2.0, 2.6rem. Updated both sidebar and bottom-dock font sliders.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: exclusive layer views + overlay toggles + smart content filtering

Layer views are now exclusive radio buttons (All, Files, Content, Work)
instead of combinatorial checkboxes. Available in both sidebar and
expanded overlay map.

Content view:
- Shows authored content nodes + file nodes they reference
- Excludes content/ tree nodes (redundant with authored nodes)
- e.g. README references src/ and kbexplorer — those show, but
  content/overview.md does NOT (it already appears as 'overview' node)

Work view:
- Shows issues, PRs, commits only
- Cross-references between issues visible as pink edges

Other changes:
- filterGraphToLayer() in types/index.ts handles per-layer logic
- isContentTreeNode() identifies redundant content/ file entries
- Layer selection persisted as single value in localStorage (kbe-layer)
- Both sidebar and overlay show the same layer selector chips

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fit-all for small layer views + fitOnStabilize takes priority over focus

When a filtered graph has <60 nodes (e.g. Content-only with ~45),
fitOnStabilize is enabled so all nodes are visible instead of focusing
on one node with everything else off-screen. Also changed the
stabilized handler so fitOnStabilize takes priority over focusNodeId —
the selected node still gets highlighted but the camera shows the
whole graph.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: brighter edge colors + thicker lines for dark background visibility

Bumped all edge colors to lighter/more saturated variants and increased
minimum width from 0.8 to 1.2. Grays that blended into dark background
(#484f58, #6e7681) replaced with lighter tones (#8b949e, #a0adb8).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: dynamic key nodes + adaptive emphasis per layer view

- KEY_NODE_IDS replaced with computeKeyNodes() — top 8 nodes by degree
  in the current (filtered) graph get a size boost
- Emphasis adapts to graph size: <30 nodes → 50% opacity with labels,
  <60 → 30% opacity, 60+ → 15% opacity (original behavior)
- Skip emphasis entirely when selected node isn't in the filtered graph
  (e.g. viewing Files layer while README is selected)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: layer toggles — show/hide file, content, work layers (#55)

Three toggle chips (Files, Content, Work) at the top of the sidebar
graph let users filter which node layers are visible. Toggling a layer
off removes its nodes and edges from the graph, minimap, and legends.
At least one layer must stay active.

- NodeLayer type + getNodeLayer() classifier in types/index.ts
- NODE_LAYER_META with labels and colors per layer
- filteredGraph memo filters nodes/edges/related by active layers
- All graph consumers (sidebar, overlay, minimap, legends) use filteredGraph
- Layer selection persisted in localStorage (kbe-layers)

Closes #55

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: edge type visual styling — colors, dashes, and legend (#56)

Each EdgeType now has a distinct visual style (color, dash pattern,
width) defined in EDGE_TYPE_STYLES. Edges in the graph use their
type's style instead of a uniform gray. The sidebar and overlay
legends show active edge types with matching line samples.

- Contains: solid gray, References: solid blue, Cross-ref: solid pink
- Frontmatter: dashed green, Mentions: dotted gray, Related: dotted dark
- Closes: solid green, Modifies: dashed amber, Imports: solid purple
- Edge tooltips now show type label (e.g. 'References: ...')
- Removed unused edgeWidth/edgeDashes options from createGraphNetwork

Closes #56

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop graph drift — increase stabilization to 500 iterations + double physics kill

Physics was likely not fully converging in 150 iterations with 150+
nodes. Increased to 500 and added stabilizationIterationsDone listener
as a second physics kill to ensure nodes stop moving.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: default zoom 1.0x, max zoom 2.0x

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reduce default zoom to 1.2x, keep 2.3x as max ceiling

1.2x shows the current node with its neighborhood visible. Users can
zoom in manually up to 2.3x via the slider. Re-center, navigation,
and initial load all consistently use 1.2x.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: consistent zoom on navigate — match initial focus scale (2.3x)

Navigation between nodes was preserving whatever zoom the user had
(net.getScale()) while initial load and re-center both used 2.3x.
Now all three use the same scale for a consistent experience.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop graph drifting — disable physics in factory + after emphasis updates

Physics is now disabled in createGraphNetwork's stabilized handler
(single source of truth) instead of scattered across HUD callers.
Also kills physics after setEmphasis() since DataSet.update() can
re-trigger the physics simulation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: re-center focuses on current node + reactive zoom sliders

Re-center button now focuses on the selected node at 2.3x (sidebar) or
2.0x (overlay) instead of fitting all nodes. Zoom sliders are now driven
by React state synced with vis-network zoom events, so dragging them
actually works.

- Re-center: focus(currentNodeId) instead of fit() (falls back to fit
  when no node selected)
- Zoom slider: sidebarZoom/overlayZoom state synced via network.on('zoom')
- Initial focus bumped from 1.8x to 2.3x for better readability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: dynamic neighborhood highlighting on node selection (#59)

When a node is selected, its direct neighbors stay fully visible while
all other nodes and edges fade to near-invisible (opacity 0.15). The
emphasis updates dynamically via setEmphasis() without rebuilding the
vis-network — smooth transitions as users navigate between nodes.

- Add setEmphasis() to GraphNetworkResult for live node/edge updates
- Sidebar graph updates emphasis on currentNodeId change (no rebuild)
- Overlay map also shows neighborhood emphasis for selected node
- Edges between non-neighbors fade to 0.08 opacity; partial edges 0.25
- Consistent 0.15 opacity for faded nodes (was 0.3, now stronger contrast)
- Focus animation slowed to 300ms for smoother panning on navigate

Closes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: zoom sliders on sidebar and overlay maps + larger overlay

- Overlay expanded to 95vw × 92vh (was 90vw × 85vh, maxWidth removed)
- Zoom slider on overlay (bottom-right): range 20-300%, smooth animation
- Zoom slider on sidebar graph (bottom): compact with −/+ labels
- Both sliders call network.moveTo() with easeInOutQuad animation

Verified with Playwright: overlay shows zoom slider, clusters legend,
re-center + close buttons. Graph renders with 13 clusters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: zoom in more on sidebar + overlay graphs, enable sidebar panning

- Sidebar graph: scale 1.8 when focused on a node (was 1.0), now
  shows current node and neighbors at readable size. Panning enabled.
- Overlay graph: fitOnStabilize uses maxZoomLevel 2.0
- Both graphs: interactive (drag to pan, scroll to zoom)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: enable panning and zoom on expanded map + zoom limits

- Expanded overlay: dragView and zoomView enabled (interactive: true)
- Sidebar minimap: stays non-interactive (click to expand)
- Zoom speed reduced to 0.3 for smoother control
- After stabilization, zoom can't go below 0.3 scale (prevents
  zooming out to nothingness)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop physics after stabilization + zoom in on re-center

- Overlay graph now stops physics after stabilization (was drifting forever)
- Overlay has its own network ref (was incorrectly using sidebar ref)
- Re-center uses maxZoomLevel: 1.5 so it zooms in rather than showing
  everything tiny

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add re-center button to constellation graph

Adds a MyLocation icon button to both:
- Sidebar graph (left/right dock) — next to the expand button
- Expanded overlay — next to the dismiss button

Calls vis-network fit() with smooth animation (400ms easeInOutQuad)
to zoom and pan to show all nodes.

Verified with Playwright: button visible in overlay as 'Re-center'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: clickable file paths (#51) + edge-weight ranked related panel (#52)

#51: File path references in code tags (src/engine/parser.ts) are now
clickable links navigating to the file's graph node. Also converts
markdown [text](node-id) links to hash-based graph navigation.
CSS: dashed underline on file links, solid on hover.

#52: Related panel now ranks by edge weight instead of raw degree.
derived_from (3.0) and references (2.0) edges now surface file nodes
that were previously hidden behind high-degree authored nodes.
Increased max from 8 to 12 related nodes.

Verified with Playwright:
- content-pipeline.md (derived_from) appears first in related panel
- src/engine/parser.ts is clickable, links to file-src/engine/parser.ts
- parser.ts appears in related panel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: typed directional edges — every edge has type, source, and weight (#49, #50)

Edge types: contains, derived_from, imports, references, frontmatter,
mentions, cross_references, modifies, closes, related

Edge sources: inline (from body links), frontmatter (YAML declared),
inferred (system-derived like containment and fuzzy matching)

Default weights per type influence layout physics:
- contains: 5.0 (tight), derived_from: 3.0, references: 2.0,
  frontmatter: 1.5, mentions: 0.5, related: 0.3

All edge creation sites updated: parseMarkdownFile, issueToNode,
splitIntoSections, loadRepoContent, buildEdges, orphan linking.

Content node spec-typed-edges.md added with full design for runtime
filtering and edge styling (Phases 2-3 for future).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop excluding content/ from file tree — authored nodes now link back to their source files

Content files were excluded from treeToNodes, so file-content/*.md nodes
didn't exist. The derived_from edges from authored nodes had nowhere to
connect. Now content/ files appear in the file tree AND as authored nodes,
linked by derived_from edges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: extract inline links and file references as graph edges

parseMarkdownFile now automatically extracts:
1. Markdown [text](target) links → edges to target nodes
2. File path references (src/..., scripts/...) → edges to file tree nodes
3. Implicit derived_from edge → links every authored node to its source file

This means content bodies that mention [Graph Engine](graph-engine) or
reference src/engine/parser.ts now create real edges in the graph without
any frontmatter declaration. The graph should be significantly denser.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: convert 33 frontmatter connections to inline links across 21 files

Applied the inline-link-extraction principle: if a connection target is
mentioned in the body text, the mention becomes an inline [link](target)
and the frontmatter connection is removed.

Result: 0 redundant frontmatter connections (was 2). 193 connections
remain in frontmatter as document-level relationships not expressed
in the body.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add inline link extraction spec node (#48)

Fundamental shift: inline links in content ARE the graph edges.
Frontmatter connections become optional/supplementary.

Spec covers: markdown links, issue refs, code imports, file containment,
edge source typing (inline/frontmatter/inferred), location tracking for
correct 1-to-N splitting, and link assessment implications.

Connected to 11 architecture nodes including content-pipeline, graph-engine,
type-system, reading-view, and all provider specs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add link assessment + multi-layer identity spec nodes (#46, #47)

Two new content nodes deeply connected to the architecture graph:

spec-link-assessment: graph health analysis CLI command
- 8 connections to architecture nodes + issue-46
- Detects orphans, broken refs, weak clusters, missing cross-refs, coverage gaps

spec-multi-layer-identity: same entity across providers
- 11 connections to architecture nodes + issue-47
- Identity URNs link file nodes, content nodes, and commit targets
- Views decide which representation to show per identity
- Link assessment detects unmapped identities

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: move specs into content/ with deep architecture + issue connections

Specs are content — moved from specs/ into content/:
- spec-providers-overview: 11 connections (3 specs + 7 architecture + 1 issue)
- spec-node-mapping: 10 connections (content-pipeline, graph-engine, type-system,
  wiki-content-modes, overview-view, reading-view, manifest-generator, issue-41)
- spec-graph-store: 11 connections (local-loader, cache-system, manifest-generator,
  type-system, graph-engine, github-api, content-pipeline, issue-42)
- spec-views: 11 connections (hud, overview-view, reading-view, graph-network,
  graph-engine, github-api, issues 43-45)

Removed specs/ top-level folder and reverted manifest generator special-case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: spec files as graph nodes + manifest reads specs/

- Added kbexplorer frontmatter to all 4 spec files so they appear as nodes
- Manifest generator now reads specs/ alongside content/
- Added fluentui-system-icons to SKIP_DIRS (prevents stack overflow)
- 4 spec nodes in 'design' cluster with cross-connections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

spec: graph provider system — tree structure with crawl/walk/run evolution

Replaced single monolithic spec with a tree:
- specs/providers/00-overview.md — current architecture analysis + evolution path
- specs/providers/01-node-mapping.md — how files become nodes (nodemap.yaml)
- specs/providers/02-graph-store.md — SQLite cache + provider interface
- specs/providers/03-views-and-expansion.md — graph views + on-demand + external

Key shift from previous design:
- Crawl-walk-run evolution of existing codebase, not a rewrite
- Node mapping (projections) moved to Phase 1, not Phase 4
- File/git providers come first (file-centric, not API-centric)
- nodemap.yaml works NOW without any provider system

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

spec: graph provider architecture — layered providers with caching and views

Design for provider-based graph system:
- Providers contribute nodes/edges with prescribed resolution depth
- Append-only SQLite graph store with provider attribution
- On-demand expansion (click to load more from any provider)
- Trigger system: providers activate based on existing graph state
- Graph views: named projections over the unified graph
- 7 providers: files, git, github, azdo, authored, projection, external
- 5 implementation phases

See specs/graph-provider-architecture.md for full design.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update README for template repo + CLI tool references

- Renamed title to kbexplorer-template
- Badge updated to GitHub Pages workflow
- Added live demo link
- 'Using in Another Repo' now references npx @anokye-labs/kbexplorer
- Removed manual setup instructions (CLI handles everything)
- Updated clone URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: clean template — remove scripts and agents moved to @anokye-labs/kbexplorer CLI

Removed:
- scripts/init.js, dev.js, build.js, transform-catalogue.js (now in CLI)
- .github/copilot/ agents, commands, skills (now bundled in CLI npm package)
- Kept: scripts/generate-manifest.js (used by Vite plugin at build time)

Renamed package to kbexplorer-template.
Added .playwright-cli/ to .gitignore.

Install the CLI: npx @anokye-labs/kbexplorer init

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/feature/kb-generate-content'

feat: add GitHub Pages deployment workflow

Deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main. Builds with local mode
(manifest + VITE_KB_LOCAL=true) and VITE_BASE_PATH=/kbexplorer/.
Copies index.html to 404.html for SPA hash-routing support.

Requires enabling GitHub Pages in repo settings (source: GitHub Actions).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: register all 2,629 Fluent UI system icons

Full Fluent icon library now available — kb-architect can pick the
best icon for any concept, not just the 70 in our vocabulary.

- NodeVisual.tsx: 2,629 React icon components registered in FLUENT_ICONS
- nodeRenderer.ts: 2,629 SVG paths for canvas rendering in minimap/graph
- Dynamic shape inference replaces hardcoded ICON_NODE_SHAPE map
  (document-like → roundedRect, structural → roundedSquare, rest → circle)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add SVG paths for all 70 Fluent icons to canvas renderer

nodeRenderer.ts now has ICON_PATHS and ICON_NODE_SHAPE entries for all
70 registered Fluent icons (was 11). The minimap/constellation graph
now renders Building, Database, Flow, Settings, Book, etc. as proper
SVG icons inside canvas nodes — not just empty shapes.

Icon paths extracted from @fluentui/react-icons via server-side rendering.
Shape assignments: circles for status/flow icons, rounded squares for
structural icons, rounded rects for document-type icons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace all emoji with Fluent UI icon names in content files

All 37 content files updated: emoji like 📖🏗️💾🔌 replaced with
registered Fluent icon names (Book, Building, Database, PlugConnected, etc.)
that render as proper Fluent UI icon components in the app.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: generate full KB content + Fluent UI icon mapping

Content generation pipeline run:
- kb-architect produced 37-node catalogue (21 existing imported, 16 new)
- 21 existing content files imported with enriched connections (153 total)
- 16 new content files generated by kb-writer with Mermaid diagrams,
  source citations, and summary tables
- Wiki files flattened from content/wiki/ to content/wiki-*.md
- config.yaml regenerated with content-only clusters (no visual/theme)

Fluent UI icon system expanded:
- NodeVisual.tsx: FLUENT_ICONS registry expanded from 11 to 70+ icons
  covering architecture, data, API, UI, security, config, testing,
  engine, docs, code, graph, visual, navigation, status categories
